description: Your rocketry club wants to break a new altitude record by converting their reliable single-stage 'Simple Model Rocket' into a two-stage vehicle. Load the base design from /home/ga/Documents/rockets/simple_model_rocket.ork and append a booster stage with an airframe (outer diameter 24.8 mm) and fins. Configure a staged motor setup using a 0-second delay motor in the booster and a standard motor with delay in the sustainer. Run a flight simulation to verify safe separation and stable flight. Save the modified two-stage design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/two_stage_upgrade.ork. Write a performance report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/staging_report.txt that includes the motors used for both stages, the simulated apogee altitude, and an explanation of why a 0-second delay motor is used in the booster.
